capability_objective: Recover safely from an uncertain mutation outcome by reading the exact resource before deciding whether a retry is safe, while avoiding similarly named resources and reporting the verified outcome in the requested order.
prompt: Cancel the meeting `Accessibility Council Check-in` (ID `cal-161`) with the reason `no longer needed for the scheduled work.` Use the provided executable calendar client against its local service; inspect the executable's help if needed. The first cancellation attempt will lose its response after the service has received it, so do not infer failure from the client error. Make one cancellation attempt, then retrieve `cal-161` directly by exact ID to resolve whether the mutation committed. Retry the cancellation only if that retrieved status is exactly `active`; do not retry if it is `cancelled` or `cancellation-pending`. Do not list or search meetings, and do not access or modify any similarly named meeting. Do not inspect or edit `.calendar/` or `.moonshiner/` directly; calendar state may be changed only through the executable client. Before responding, write the same response text to `completion.txt`. The response and `completion.txt` must each contain exactly three non-empty lines: identify the target by title and ID first, give its verified final status second, and say whether a retry occurred last.
